_comment: Reflected catalog of Dynatrace Terraform-provider resource types that dt-pilot ships scaffolds for. Sync-TerraformCatalog.ps1 reads this file and regenerates modules/terraform/configs/<family>/<resource-name-minus-dynatrace-prefix>/. Adding a row is the cheapest way to extend Terraform scaffold coverage; keep the catalog curated and prefer the _v2 settings-2.0 resource shapes. Each commonVariable may carry an optional providerArgument that maps the dt-pilot variable name to the provider's actual top-level argument; when omitted the scaffold falls back to '<name> = var.<name>' with a TODO marker (use this for variables that feed nested blocks or have no clean top-level analogue).

resources.summary: Settings 2.0 management zone with attribute / dimension rules. Most downstream alerting / SLO / dashboard filters reference a management zone, so this is usually the first resource a new project adopts.

commonVariables.name: zone_name
commonVariables.description: Display name for the management zone.

resources.summary: Settings 2.0 auto-tag rule. Tags become the primary input to downstream management-zone and alerting filters, so auto-tags usually deploy before everything else that references them.

commonVariables.name: tag_name
commonVariables.description: Tag key (the right-hand side will come from the rule expression).

commonVariables.name: rule_expression
commonVariables.description: DQL-like or attribute predicate that selects the entities to tag. Feeds a nested rules { } block -- no top-level provider argument.

resources.summary: Severity-rule set that gates which problems trigger notifications. Almost always scoped to a management zone via the management_zone attribute.

commonVariables.name: profile_name
commonVariables.description: Display name for the alerting profile.

commonVariables.name: management_zone_id
commonVariables.description: ID of the management zone this profile is scoped to (use a resource attribute reference, not a literal MZ-id).

resources.summary: Settings 2.0 SLO. evaluation_type is AGGREGATE or EVENT_BASED with different required fields per type. The warning threshold MUST be less strict than the target (lower percentage) so warnings fire before the SLO breaches.

commonVariables.name: target_pct
commonVariables.description: Target percentage (0-100). Validation lives in the consuming variables.tf.

commonVariables.name: warning_pct
commonVariables.description: Warning percentage (0-100). MUST be lower than target_pct.

commonVariables.name: management_zone_id
commonVariables.description: ID of the management zone the SLO filters on. Used in the filter expression (e.g. 'type(SERVICE),mzId(${var.management_zone_id})') -- no top-level provider argument.

resources.summary: Routes problems matching an alerting profile to email / Slack / webhook / PagerDuty / Jira / OpsGenie / xMatters. The per-channel payload lives in nested blocks (email {} / slack {} / etc.); generate the schema or read the provider docs before authoring.

commonVariables.name: alerting_profile_id
commonVariables.description: ID of the alerting profile this notification fires against.

commonVariables.name: recipient_email
commonVariables.description: Email recipient. Feeds the nested email { to = [...] } block -- no top-level provider argument.

resources.summary: Dynatrace dashboard payload. Templates tend to be large -- prefer storing the bulk as a JSON file referenced via file() rather than inlining megabytes of HCL.

commonVariables.name: dashboard_name
commonVariables.description: Display name shown in the Dynatrace UI. Lives in the nested dashboard_metadata { name = ... } block -- no top-level provider argument.

commonVariables.name: owner
commonVariables.description: Owner email or service-account identifier. Lives in the nested dashboard_metadata { owner = ... } block -- no top-level provider argument.
